text: respectful constructive discussions Welcome contributors regardless background experience Focus code, person Provide helpful feedback open receiving Report inappropriate behavior maintainers

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: R 3.5.0 later Git installed configured GitHub account Basic knowledge R Git workflows

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Fork repository GitHub Clone fork locally: Create feature branch: Set Git hooks (optional recommended):
code: git clone https://github.com/YOUR-USERNAME/Useful-R-Programs.git cd Useful-R-Programs git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name # Create a pre-commit hook to check your code

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Check existing issues avoid duplicates Search closed issues case ’s already resolved Verify issue occurs latest version

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Please include: Clear title - Briefly describe issue Description - Detailed explanation problem R version - Output R.version Environment - Operating system (Windows/Mac/Linux) Reproduction steps: Expected behavior - happen Actual behavior - actually happens Error messages - Full error output applicable Screenshots - relevant issue
code: # Minimal reproducible example source("script-name.R") function_call()  # What triggers the issue

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: bug - Something isn’t working enhancement - Feature request improvement documentation - Issues docs examples good-first-issue - Good newcomers help-wanted - Extra attention needed question - General questions

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Branch naming conventions: - feature/ - New functionality - fix/ - Bug fix - docs/ - Documentation - refactor/ - Code restructuring (behavior change) - test/ - Adding updating tests
code: git checkout -b feature/descriptive-name

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Single Responsibility: change address one issue/feature Keep focused: Avoid mixing unrelated changes File organization: Follow existing structure

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: submitting: Important: Test across different scenarios: - valid inputs - edge cases - invalid/missing inputs - clean R session
code: # Load and test your modified script source("modified-script.R")  # Test the interactive prompts function_name()  # Verify no errors or warnings

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: See Commit Message Guidelines .
code: git add . git commit -m "feat: Add descriptive message"

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
code: git push origin feature/your-feature-name

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Go GitHub click “New Pull Request” Select fork branch Title - Clear, descriptive title Description - changes, , issues fixes Type - Bug fix, feature, enhancement, documentation Related Issues - Link related issues (#123) Checklist - Confirm ’ve followed guidelines

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Scripts organized functionality following directories: Package Management (package-management/) - Package removal, auditing, scanning, cleanup Memory Management (memory-management/) - Memory cleanup, cache sweeping, garbage collection Development Environment (dev-environment/) - Git setup, tool bootstrapping, pre-commit hooks, session logging Session Management (session-management/) - State auditing, function masking detection, environment snapshots Batch Processing (batch-processing/) - Memory-safe loops, checkpoint recovery, network operations Code Generation (code-generation/) - Release automation, vignette scaffolding, test generation, clean-room testing Privacy (privacy/) - Data anonymization, PII removal, pseudonymization Utilities (utilities/) - Miscellaneous utilities specialized tools adding new scripts: 1. Choose appropriate directory 2. creating new category, create new directory 3. Include README.md directory explaining contents 4. Update main README.md links new directory 5. Reference scripts appropriate section

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
code: # Spacing around operators x <- 1 + 2  # Good x<-1+2      # Bad  # Function calls result <- my_function(arg1, arg2) result <- my_function(   arg1 = value1,   arg2 = value2 )  # If statements if (condition) {   # Code } else {   # Code }  # Loops for (i in seq_len(n)) {   # Code }

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Add roxygen-style comments:
code: #' Brief description of function #' #' Longer explanation of what the function does, when to use it, #' and any important side effects. #' #' @param arg_name Description of argument #' @param arg_name2 Description of second argument #' #' @return Description of what is returned #' #' @examples #' \dontrun{ #'   my_function(arg_name = value) #' } #' #' @export  my_function <- function(arg_name, arg_name2 = default) {   # Implementation }

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
code: # Use clear, descriptive comments # Explain the "why", not the "what"  # Bad: x <- x + 1  # Add 1 to x  # Good: # Increment counter for next iteration x <- x + 1  # Multi-line comment for complex logic # Check if threshold exceeded before proceeding # This prevents unnecessary processing if (value > threshold) {   # Process }

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
code: # Include helpful error messages if (!file.exists(filename)) {   stop(sprintf("File not found: '%s'. Please check the path.", filename)) }  # Use informative warnings if (length(items) == 0) {   warning("No items to process. Returning empty result.")   return(invisible(NULL)) }  # Use tryCatch for recovery result <- tryCatch({   risky_operation() }, error = function(e) {   message(sprintf("Error during processing: %s", e$message))   return(NULL) })

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: using select.list() readline():
code: # Provide clear prompts choice <- select.list(   choices = c("Option A", "Option B", "Cancel"),   title = "What would you like to do?" )  # Validate input threshold <- readline(prompt = "Enter threshold (default: 50): ") threshold <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(threshold)) if (is.na(threshold)) {   message("Invalid input. Using default value of 50.")   threshold <- 50 }

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Use following format commit messages:
code: <type>: <subject>  <body>  <footer>

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: feat - New feature fix - Bug fix docs - Documentation style - Code style changes (formatting, missing semicolons, etc.) refactor - Code refactoring without behavior change test - Adding updating tests chore - Maintenance tasks

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Use imperative mood (“add” “added”) Don’t capitalize first letter period end Limit 50 characters

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Explain , Wrap 72 characters Separate subject blank line Use bullet points multiple changes

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Reference issues: Fixes #123, Closes #456 Note breaking changes: BREAKING CHANGE: description

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
code: feat: add memory_sweeper function  Add interactive function to clean large objects from global environment. Includes threshold configuration and garbage collection trigger.  Fixes #45 fix: prevent infinite loop in network_diplomat  Add max_retries check to prevent infinite retry loops when server is unresponsive.  Closes #78 docs: update README with usage examples  Add clear examples for each script category to help new users get started quickly.

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: New functions Bug fixes (add test reproduces bug first) Important features Edge cases error conditions

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
code: # Example test structure test_that("function handles missing input", {   expect_error(     my_function(missing_arg),     "required argument"   ) })  test_that("function returns expected output", {   result <- my_function(valid_input)   expect_type(result, "list")   expect_length(result, 3) })

title: Contributing to Useful R Programs
text: Correctness - work intended? Code Quality - Follows style guidelines best practices Documentation - Clear comments function documentation Testing - Tested thoroughly, including edge cases Breaking Changes - Clearly documented backward compatible Performance - unnecessary slowdowns

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: devkit zero-dependency toolkit designed assist R package developers data scientists maintaining high standards code quality, session reproducibility, system efficiency. guide provides overview toolkit’s core modules integrate workflow.

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: Maintaining clean DESCRIPTION file critical CRAN compliance. audit_dependencies(): Scans R/ tests/ directories ensure used packages declared DESCRIPTION. scan_dependencies(): Identifies packages currently attached session actually used code. remove_package(): Safely removes package checking orphan dependencies. remove_user_installed_packages(): Cleans user-installed packages preserving base recommended packages.

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: Reduce boilerplate avoid manual errors automated generators. architect_release(): Interactively bumps package version generates NEWS.md entry. architect_vignette(): Creates CRAN-compliant RMarkdown vignette structure. scaffold_tests(): Generates testthat boilerplate functions. scaffold_parallel(): Generates necessary code set parallel cluster.

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: Ensure scripts don’t leave user’s environment messy state. audit_script(): Captures state options(), par(), getwd() script runs, prompting revert changes. detect_masking(): Identifies functions different packages share name helps lock priority. export_snapshot(): Creates script recreate current session’s package environment.

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: simulate_clean_room(): Runs script completely vanilla R session (--vanilla) ensure doesn’t rely hidden local state. benchmark_branches(): Compares execution time script across different Git branches quantify performance gains.

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: Prevent R crashing large-scale data processing. sweep_memory(): Interactively identifies removes large objects global environment. hunt_zombies(): Cleans orphaned graphics devices temporary files. sweep_temp_cache(): Flushes hidden temporary caches (e.g., knitr, raster).

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: loop_guardian(): Wraps long loops memory monitor alerts hit RAM limit. dispatch_checkpoints(): Implements save--resume system batch processing, protecting work crashes.

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: mask_identity(): guided workflow scramble drop PII columns dataframe preserving statistical distributions.

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: dictate_dictionary(): Interactively generates roxygen2 @format block datasets, ensuring data dictionaries professional complete.

title: Getting Started with devkit
text: network_diplomat(): wrapper network requests implements exponential backoff respects rate limits (HTTP 429).

title: Authors and Citation
text: Zankrut Goyani. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Goyani Z (2026). devkit: R Development Toolkit. R package version 0.0.1, https://zankrut20.github.io/devkit/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {devkit: R Development Toolkit},   author = {Zankrut Goyani},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.1},   url = {https://zankrut20.github.io/devkit/}, }

title: R Development Toolkit
text: devkit professional, zero-dependency R development toolkit designed streamline package management, environment setup, session auditing, batch processing. provides suite utilities help developers maintain CRAN compliance optimize development workflow.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: can install devkit directly GitHub:
code: # Install devtools if not already installed if (!require("devtools")) install.packages("devtools")  # Install devkit devtools::install_github("zankrut20/devkit")

title: R Development Toolkit
text: installed, simply load library access utilities:
code: library(devkit)  # Example: Audit your package dependencies audit_dependencies()  # Example: Clean up memory hogs hunt_zombies()

title: R Development Toolkit
text: audit_dependencies(): Verifies DESCRIPTION file vs actual code usage. remove_package(): Smart package removal orphan dependency checking. remove_user_installed_packages(): Cleans user-installed packages preserving base/recommended ones. scan_dependencies(): Identifies unused packages session.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: sweep_memory(): Removes large objects triggers garbage collection. hunt_zombies(): Cleans hidden temp files orphaned graphics devices. sweep_temp_cache(): Flushes R session caches across system.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: bootstrap_dev_env(): Installs attaches core dev tools. manage_deprecation(): Scaffolds deprecation wrappers refactors calls. setup_preflight(): Configures Git pre-commit hooks safety. setup_sentinel(): Enables dual-logging session reproducibility.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: audit_script(): Snapshots session state script execution. detect_masking(): Resolves namespace conflicts function masking. export_snapshot(): Exports attached packages installation script.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: dispatch_checkpoints(): Crash-resilient batch processing recovery. loop_guardian(): Memory-safe iteration RAM monitoring. network_diplomat(): Rate-limited network requests exponential backoff.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: architect_release(): Automates version bumping release notes. architect_vignette(): Scaffolds CRAN-compliant vignettes. scaffold_parallel(): Generates parallel processing boilerplate. scaffold_tests(): Generates testthat boilerplate. simulate_clean_room(): Verifies script reproducibility vanilla session.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: mask_identity(): Interactively anonymizes PII datasets.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: benchmark_branches(): Compares performance across Git branches. dictate_dictionary(): Generates roxygen2 documentation data frames.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: package licensed MIT License.

title: R Development Toolkit
text: Contributions welcome! Please see CONTRIBUTING.md guidelines.

title: Checkpoint Dispatcher — dispatch_checkpoints
text: crash-resilient wrapper batch processing silently caches progress defined intervals interactively recovers interrupted sessions.

title: Checkpoint Dispatcher — dispatch_checkpoints
code: dispatch_checkpoints(   items,   target_func,   checkpoint_file = "batch_checkpoint.rds" )

title: Checkpoint Dispatcher — dispatch_checkpoints
text: items vector list items process. target_func function apply item. checkpoint_file Character. file path state cache. Defaults `"batch_checkpoint.rds"`.

title: Checkpoint Dispatcher — dispatch_checkpoints
text: list successfully processed results, invisible list status = "cancelled" aborted resumption.

title: Checkpoint Dispatcher — dispatch_checkpoints
text: function provides safety layer long-running batch operations: Checks existing `.rds` checkpoint file. found, prompts user       resume last saved index restart process. Prompts user save frequency (e.g., every 100 items). Executes `target_func` item `items` within `tryCatch` block. critical error occurs, immediately saves current state       `checkpoint_file` halts execution. Periodically saves state based specified frequency. Upon successful completion items, temporary checkpoint file deleted.

title: Zombie Data Hunter — hunt_zombies
text: Hunts "invisible" memory hogs massive temporary files, unclosed graphical devices, uncollected garbage, prompting user targeted cleaning reclaim system resources.

title: Zombie Data Hunter — hunt_zombies
code: hunt_zombies()

title: Zombie Data Hunter — hunt_zombies
text: Invisibly returns named list components: status ("done" "cancelled"), actions_taken (character vector), temp_flushed_mb (numeric), devices_closed (integer).

title: Zombie Data Hunter — hunt_zombies
text: function performs system scan three types "zombie" data: Temporary Files: Calculates total size current       session's temporary directory. exceeds 5 MB, offers flush . Graphical Devices: Checks open graphical devices       (e.g., PDF, PNG, RStudioGD) offers close . Orphaned Memory: Offers perform "deep" garbage collection       calling `gc()` twice, forces R clear older newer       generations memory.

title: Bulk Loop Guardian — loop_guardian
text: memory-safe wrapper heavy iterations monitors RAM usage triggers interactive failsafe environment approaches critical capacity.

title: Bulk Loop Guardian — loop_guardian
code: loop_guardian(   items,   target_func,   limit_mb = 4000,   save_path = "emergency_checkpoint.rds" )

title: Bulk Loop Guardian — loop_guardian
text: items vector list items process. target_func function apply item. limit_mb Numeric. memory limit megabytes triggering alarm. Defaults `4000`. save_path Character. dump emergency checkpoint data. Defaults `"emergency_checkpoint.rds"`.

title: Bulk Loop Guardian — loop_guardian
text: list successfully processed results.

title: Bulk Loop Guardian — loop_guardian
text: function provides safeguard memory-related crashes large-scale batch processing: Iterates `items` vector, applying `target_func` element. Every 50 iterations, checks current memory usage using `gc()`. memory usage exceeds `limit_mb`, first attempts deep garbage       collection recover RAM. memory remains threshold GC, triggers interactive       alarm, allowing user : Save current progress `save_path` abort. Ignore limit attempt continue. Abort immediately without saving.

title: Bulk Loop Guardian — loop_guardian
text: function modifies files disk global environment. Please ensure backup using version control (e.g., Git) execution.

title: Interactive Lifecycle Manager — manage_deprecation
code: manage_deprecation()

title: Interactive Lifecycle Manager — manage_deprecation
text: Invisibly returns named list components: status ("done", "cancelled", "error"), old_function, new_function, wrapper_file, replacements (integer count).

title: Interactive Lifecycle Manager — manage_deprecation
text: function manages deprecation lifecycle following steps: Prompts user name function deprecated replacement. title: The Network Diplomat — network_diplomat
text: Safely executes network requests across list URLs endpoints, ensuring server politeness interactive rate limiting resilience via automatic retries exponential backoff.

title: The Network Diplomat — network_diplomat
code: network_diplomat(targets, target_func, max_retries = 3)

title: The Network Diplomat — network_diplomat
text: targets vector URLs target IDs process. target_func function makes network request (takes one target). max_retries Integer. Maximum number times retry single failure. Defaults `3`.

title: The Network Diplomat — network_diplomat
text: list successfully processed results, `NA` permanent failures.

title: The Network Diplomat — network_diplomat
text: function implements robust network request manager: Rate Limiting: Prompts user server's requests-per-minute       limit calculates precise sleep interval requests avoid       blocked. Retry Logic: Wraps request `tryCatch` block. request       fails, retry `max_retries` times. Exponential Backoff: failure, function waits       increasing amount time (5s, 10s, 20s, etc.) retrying. HTTP 429 Handling: "Many Requests" (HTTP 429) error       detected, adds additional penalty delay backoff time. Graceful Failure: retries exhausted, target       marked `NA` process continues next target.
